Tunisia House Price Predictor is a full-stack application that estimates home prices in Tunisia using a Linear Regression model. The frontend is built with React and Vite, and the backend is built with FastAPI using a hexagonal architecture. The model is serialized with Safetensors and metadata files, with feature ordering governed by columns.json.

The production deployment target is Railway, where a single container serves both static frontend assets and backend API endpoints. The system is designed so model loading occurs at application startup and inference remains fast while non-critical persistence writes can run asynchronously.

Core API endpoints include health, metadata, and prediction routes under /api/v1. Metadata provides supported locations and model information used by the frontend form. Prediction requests include area, rooms, bathrooms, age, and location.

Project documentation and implementation details are available in README.md and docs/IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md. Training and export artifacts are maintained in the machine learning pipeline and artifacts directory to preserve inference compatibility.
